verses: śrī⋅lalitā⋅devyādi⋅aṣṭa⋅sakhī⋅vandanā jāṁ kām⋅api vraja⋅kule vṛṣabhānu⋅jāyāḥ prekṣya sva⋅pakṣa⋅padavīm anuruddhyamānām sadyas tadiṣṭa⋅ghaṭanena kṛtārthayantīṁ devīṁ guṇaiḥ sulalitāṁ lalitāṁ namāmi śrī⋅lalitāṣṭakama lalitā ca viśākhā ca citrā campaka⋅latikā raṅga⋅devī sudevī ca tuṅga⋅vidyā indurekhikā etābhyohaṣṭa⋅sakhībhyaśca satatañca namo namaḥ tathāpi mama sarvasvā lalitā sarva⋅vanditā kāruṇya⋅kalpa⋅latike! lalite! namaste rādhā⋅samāna⋅guṇa⋅cāturike! viśākhe! tvāṁ naumi campaka⋅late’cyuta⋅cita⋅caure vande vicitra⋅carite! sakhi! citra⋅lekhe! śrī⋅raṅgadevi! dayite! praṇayāṅga⋅raṅge! tubhyaṁ namo’stu sukhade! dayite! sudevi! vidyāvinoda⋅sadane’pi ca tuṅgavidye! pūrṇendu⋅khaṇḍa⋅nakhare sumukhīndulekhe śrī⋅rūpādi mañjarī⋅vandanā tāmbūlārpaṇa⋅pāda⋅mardana⋅payodānābhisārādibhir⋅ vṛndāraṇya⋅maheśvarīṁ priyatayā jās toṣayanti priyāḥ prāṇa⋅preṣṭha⋅sakhī⋅kulādapi kilāsaṅkocita⋅bhūmikāḥ keli⋅bhūmiṣu rūpa⋅mañjarī⋅mukhās tā dāsikāḥ saṁśraye śrī⋅vraja⋅vilāsa⋅stavaḥ śrī⋅śrī⋅rādhikā⋅vandanā mahā⋅bhāva⋅svarūpā tvaṁ kṛṣṇa⋅priyā⋅varīyasī prema⋅bhakti⋅prade! devi! rādhike tvāṁ namāmyaham rādhe vṛndāvanādhīśe! karuṇāmṛta⋅vāhini kṛpayā nija⋅pādābja dāsyaṁ mahyaṁ pradīyatām bhajāmi rādhām⋅aravinda⋅netrāṁ, smarāmi rādhāṁ madhura⋅smitāsyām vadāmi rādhāṁ karuṇābharārdrāṁ tato mamānyāsti gatir⋅na kāpi śrī⋅viśākhānanda⋅dābhidha⋅stotram hā devi! kāku⋅bhara⋅gadgada⋅jāḍya⋅vācā jāce nipatya bhuvi daṇḍavadudbhaṭārtiḥ asya prasādamabudhasya janasya kṛtbā gāndharvike nija⋅gaṇe gaṇanāṁ vidhehi śrī⋅gāndharvā⋅saṁprārthanāṣṭakam pādābjayostava vinā vara⋅dāsyameva nānyat kadāpi samaye kila devi jāce sakhyāya te mama namo’stu namo’stu nityaṁ dāsyāya te mama raso’stu raso’stu satyam vilāpa⋅kusumāñjaliḥ rādhāṁ rāseśvarīṁ ramyāṁ govinda⋅mohinī parām kṛṣṇabhānū⋅sutāṁ devīṁ namāmi śrī⋅hari⋅priyām rāsotsaba⋅vilāsini! namaste parameṣvari! kṛṣṇa⋅prāṇādhike rādhe! paramānanda⋅vigrahe amala⋅kamala⋅kāntiṁ nīla⋅vastrāṁ sukeśīṁ śaśa⋅dhara⋅sama⋅vaktrāṁ khañjanākṣīṁ manojñām stana⋅yuga⋅gata⋅muktā⋅dāma⋅dīptāṁ kiśorīṁ vraja⋅pati⋅suta⋅kāntāṁ rādhikām āśraye’ham śrī⋅stava⋅mālā jat⋅kiṅkarīṣu bahuśaḥ khalu kāku⋅vāṇī nityaṁ parasya puruṣasya śikhaṇḍa⋅mauleḥ tasyāḥ kadā rasa⋅nidher vṛṣabhānu⋅jāyās tat⋅keli⋅kuñja⋅bhavanāṅgana⋅mārjanī syām? śrī⋅rādhā⋅rasa⋅sudhā⋅nidhi śrī⋅bāla⋅gopāla⋅vandanā karāravindena padāravindaṁ mukhāravinde viniveśayantam vrajeśvarī⋅kroḍa⋅gataṁ⋅hasantaṁ bālaṁ mukundaṁ manasā smarāmi navīna⋅nīrada⋅śyāmaṁ nīlendīvara⋅locanam jaśodā⋅nandanaṁ naumi kṛṣṇaṁ gopāla⋅rūpiṇam nārada⋅pañcarātre gopāla⋅stotram goṣṭheśvarī⋅vadana⋅phutkṛti lolanetraṁ jānu⋅dvayena dharaṇī⋅manusañcaran⋅tam kiñcinnava⋅smita⋅sudhā⋅madhurādharābhaṁ bālaṁ tamāla⋅dala⋅nīla⋅mahaṁ bhajāmi padyāvalī 132 sajala⋅jalada⋅nīla⋅nyakṛta⋅śyāma⋅lāṅgaṁ kara⋅tala⋅dhṛta⋅śailaṁ veṇu⋅vādyānuśīlam madhura⋅madhura⋅līlaṁ śrīla gopāla⋅mallaṁ vraja⋅jana⋅kula⋅pālaṁ dhīmahi brahma⋅mūlam dadhi⋅mathananinādais⋅tyakta⋅nidraḥ prabhāte nibhṛta⋅padamagāraṁ vallavīnāṁ praviṣṭaḥ mukha⋅kamala⋅samīvairāśu nirvāpya dīpān kavalita navanītaḥ pātu māṁ bāla⋅kṛṣṇaḥ padyāvalī 142 savya pāṇau niyam⋅itaravaṁ kiṅkiṇī⋅dāmadhṛtvā kubjībhūya prapada⋅gatibhirmanda⋅mandaṁ vihasya akṣnorbhaṅgyā vihasita⋅mukhīrvārayan samukhīnā mātuḥ paścād⋅aharata harirjātu haiyaṅgavīnam śrī⋅śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅vandanā namo brahmaṇya⋅devāya go⋅brāhmaṇa⋅hitāya ca jagad⋅dhitāya kṛṣṇāya govindāya namo namaḥ viṣṇu⋅purāṇa bhava⋅bhayam⋅apahantuṁ jñāna⋅vijñāna⋅sāraṁ nigama⋅kṛd⋅upajahre bhṛṅga⋅vad veda⋅sāram amṛtam⋅udadhitaś⋅cāpāyayad bhṛtya⋅vargān puruṣam⋅ṛṣabham⋅ādyaṁ kṛṣṇa⋅saṁjñaṁ nato’smi śrī.bhā. 11.29.49 phullendīvara⋅kāntim indu⋅vadanaṁ varhāvataṁsa⋅priyaṁ śrīvatsāṅkam⋅udāra⋅kaustubha⋅dharaṁ pītāmbara⋅sundaram gopīnāṁ nayanot⋅palārcita⋅tanuṁ go⋅gopa⋅saṅghāvṛtaṁ govindaṁ kala⋅veṇu⋅vādana⋅paraṁ⋅divyāṅga⋅bhūṣaṁ bhaje kastūrī⋅tilakaṁ lalāṭa⋅paṭale vakṣaḥ⋅sthale kaustubhaṁ nāsāgre vara⋅mauktikaṁ kara⋅tale veṇuḥ kare kaṅkaṇam sarvāṅge hari⋅candanaṁ sulalitaṁ kaṇṭhe ca muktāvalī gopa⋅strī⋅pariveṣṭito vijayate gopāla⋅cūḍāmaṇiḥ śrī⋅gopāla⋅sahasra⋅nāma⋅stotram varhāpīḍābhirāmaṁ mṛga⋅mada⋅tilakaṁ kuṇḍalākrānta⋅gaṇḍaṁ kañjākṣaṁ kambu⋅kaṇṭhaṁ smita⋅subhaga⋅mukhaṁ svādhare nyasta⋅veṇum śyāmaṁ śāntaṁ tribhaṅgaṁ ravi⋅kara⋅vasanaṁ bhūṣitaṁ vaijayantyā vande vṛndāvana⋅sthaṁ juvatī⋅śata⋅vṛtaṁ brahma⋅gopāla⋅veśam śrī⋅govindaṁ ghanaśyāmaṁ pītāmbara⋅dharaṁ paraṁ śrī⋅nanda⋅nandanaṁ naumi śrī⋅gopī⋅jana⋅vallabhaṁ kṛṣṇāya kṛṣṇa⋅candrāya vṛndāvana⋅vihāraṇe namaste ballvīśāya rādhikā⋅pataye namaḥ kandarpa⋅koṭi⋅ramyāya sphurad⋅indīvara⋅tviṣe jaganmohana⋅līlāya namo gopendra⋅sunave vaṁśī⋅nyastāsya⋅candraṁ smita⋅yutam atulaṁ pīta⋅vastraṁ vareṇyaṁ kañjākṣaṁ sarva⋅dakṣaṁ nava⋅ghana⋅sadṛśaṁ varha⋅cūḍaṁ śaraṇyam trai⋅bhaṅgair bhaṅgimāṅgaṁ vraja⋅yuvati⋅yutaṁ dhvasta⋅keśyādi⋅śūraṁ vande śrī⋅nanda⋅sūnuṁ madhura⋅rasa⋅tanuṁ dhurya⋅mādhurya⋅pūram namo nalina⋅netrāya veṇu⋅vādya⋅vinodine rādhādhara⋅sudhā⋅pāna⋅śāline vana⋅māline kṛṣṇāya vāsudevāya haraye paramātmane praṇata⋅kleśa⋅nāśāya govindāya namo namaḥ śrī.bhā. 10.73.16 śrī⋅śrī⋅rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅vandanā kanaka⋅jalada⋅gātrau nīla⋅śoṇābja netrau mṛgamada⋅vara⋅bhālau mālatī⋅kunda⋅mālau tarala⋅taruṇa⋅veśau nīla⋅pītāmbareśau smara nibhṛta⋅nikuñje rādhikā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅candrau śrī⋅nikuñja⋅rahasya⋅stavaḥ aṅga⋅śyāmalima⋅cchaṭābhir⋅abhito mandī⋅kṛtendīvaraṁ jāḍyaṁ jāguḍa⋅rociṣāṁ vidadhataṁ paṭṭāmbarasya śriyā vṛndāraṇya⋅nivāsinaṁ hṛdi lasad⋅dāmābhir āmodaraṁ rādhā⋅skandha⋅niveśitojvala⋅bhujaṁ dhyāyema dāmodaram śrī⋅vṛndāvana⋅dhāma⋅vandanā jayati jayati vṛndāraṇyam⋅etan murāreḥ priyatamam⋅ati⋅sādhusvānta⋅vaikunṭha⋅vāsāt ramayati sa sadā gāḥ pālayan jatra gopīḥ svarita⋅madhura⋅veṇur vardhayan prema rāse śrī⋅bṛhad⋅bhāgavatāmṛtama ānanda⋅vṛnda⋅paritundilamindirāyā ānanda⋅vṛnda⋅parinandita⋅nandaputraṁ govinda⋅sundara⋅vadhū⋅parinanditaṁ tad⋅ vṛndāvanaṁ madhura⋅mūrtamahaṁ namāmi śrī⋅śrī⋅balarāma⋅vandanā namaste tu hala⋅grāma namaste muṣalāyudha namaste revatīkānta namaste bhakta⋅vatsala namaste balināṁ śreṣṭha namaste dharaṇī⋅dhara pralambāre namaste tu trāhi māṁ kṛṣṇa⋅pūrvaja śrī⋅śrī⋅govardhana⋅vandanā hantāyam⋅adrir⋅abalā hari⋅dāsa⋅varyo jad⋅rāma⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caraṇa⋅sparśa⋅pramodaḥ mānaṁ tanoti saha⋅go⋅gaṇayos⋅tayor⋅yat pānīya⋅suyavasa⋅kandara⋅kanda⋅mūlaiḥ śrī.bhā. 10.21.18 govardhano jayati śaila⋅kulādhirājo jo gopikābhir udito hari⋅dāsa⋅varyaḥ kṛṣṇena śakra⋅makha⋅bhaṅga⋅kṛtārcito jaḥ saptāhamasya kara⋅padma⋅tale’vātsīt śrī⋅bṛhad bhāgavatāmṛtama saptāham⋅evācyuta⋅hasta⋅paṅkaje bhṛṅgāya⋅mānaṁ phala⋅mūla⋅kandaraiḥ saṁsevyamānaṁ harim⋅ātma⋅vṛndakair⋅ govardhanādriṁ śirasā namāmi śrī⋅śrī⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍa⋅vandanā ananta⋅hari⋅rādhikā⋅madhura⋅keli⋅vṛndaiḥ sadā mahādbhuta⋅maho! mahā⋅rasa⋅camatkṛtīnāṁ nidhim mahojvalaṁ mahā⋅susaurabhatamaṁ ca vṛndāvane samaronmada⋅tadīśvarī⋅dayita⋅divya⋅kuṇḍaṁ numaḥ śrī⋅vṛndāvana⋅mahimāmṛtama he śrī⋅sarovara! sadā tvayi sā mad⋅īśā preṣṭhena sārdham iha khelati kāma⋅raṅgaiḥ tvañcet priyāt priyamatīva tayor⋅itī māṁ hā darśayādya kṛpayā mama jīvitaṁ tām śrī⋅vilāpa⋅kusumāñjaliḥ jathā rādhā priyā viṣṇos⋅tasyāḥ kuṇḍaṁ priyaṁ tathā sarva⋅gopīṣu saivaikā viṣṇor⋅atyanta⋅vallabhā śrī⋅viṣṇu⋅purāṇa śrī⋅śyāma⋅kuṇḍa⋅vandanā duṣṭāriṣṭa⋅vadhe svayaṁ sama⋅bhavat kṛṣṇāṅghri⋅padmādidaṁ sphītaṁ jan⋅makaranda vistṛtirivāriṣṭākhyam⋅iṣṭaṁ saraḥ sopānaiḥ parirañjitaṁ priyatayā śrī⋅rādhayā kāritaiḥ premnāliṅgadiva priyā⋅sara idaṁ tannitya⋅nityaṁ bhaje śrī⋅vraja⋅vilāsa⋅stavaḥ śrī⋅yamunā⋅vandanā cid⋅ānanda⋅bhānoḥ sadā⋅nanda⋅sūnoḥ para⋅prema⋅pātrī drava brahma⋅gātrī aghānāṁ lavitrī jagat⋅kṣema⋅dhātrī pavitrī⋅kriyān no vapur⋅mitra⋅putrī śrī⋅padma⋅purāṇa gaṅgādi⋅tīrtha⋅pariṣevita⋅pāda⋅padmāṁ go⋅loka⋅saukhya⋅rasa⋅pūramahiṁ mahimnā āplāvitākhila⋅sudhā⋅sujalāṁ sukhābdhau rādhā⋅mukunda⋅muditāṁ jamunāṁ namāmi śrī⋅gaṅgā⋅vandanā sadyaḥ pātaka⋅saŋhantrī sadeyā duḥkha⋅vināśinī sukhadā mokṣadā gaṅgā gaṅgaiva paramā gatiḥ
en_translation:
I offer obeisances to Lalitā-devī, who is beautifully adorned with all good qualities. Seeing Rādhā on the path of the party of Kṛṣṇa, she immediately makes Her successful by fulfilling Her desire.

Lalitā, Viśākhā, Citrā, Campakalatā, Raṅgadevī, Sudevī, Tuṅgavidyā, and Indurekhā. I offer my obeisances again and again to these eight Sakhīs. Still, Lalitā is my all-in-all, worshipped by everyone.

O wish-fulfilling creeper of mercy, Lalitā! Obeisances to you. O Viśākhā, possessing cleverness and qualities equal to Rādhā! I praise you, O Campakalatā, who steals the mind of Acyuta! I bow to you, O friend Citralekhā, of wonderful character!

O Śrī Raṅgadevī! O beloved! O stage for the limbs of love! Let there be obeisances to you, O giver of happiness, O beloved Sudevī! O Tuṅgavidyā, even in the abode of knowledge and pleasure! O Indulekhā, whose nails are like pieces of the full moon and who has a beautiful face!

I take shelter of the maidservants headed by Rūpa Mañjarī in the play-grounds. They please the Great Queen of Vṛndāvana (Rādhā) with affection by offering betel, massaging Her feet, giving water, and arranging trysts—tasks which are unhesitatingly performed even by the group of Sakhīs who are dearer to Her than life.

You are the form of Mahābhāva, the best of the beloveds of Kṛṣṇa. O Devi Rādhikā, bestower of loving devotion, I bow to You.

O Rādhe, Queen of Vṛndāvana, River of the nectar of compassion! Please mercifully give me the service of Your own lotus feet.

I worship Rādhā who has lotus eyes; I remember Rādhā who has a sweet smile on Her face; I speak of Rādhā who is melted by the burden of compassion. Therefore, I have no other goal.

Alas, O Devi! Today, with a voice choked with piteous words, falling on the ground like a stick, I beg with intense distress: bestowing mercy on this ignorant person, O Gāndharvikā, please count me among Your own people.

O Devi! I indeed never beg for anything else at any time except for the excellent service of Your lotus feet. Obeisances to Your friendship; obeisances always. May my taste be for Your service; may it be true.

I bow to the Goddess Śrī Hari-priyā, Rādhā, the Queen of the Rāsa dance, beautiful, the supreme enchantress of Govinda, the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu.

O You who enjoy the festival of the Rāsa dance! Obeisances to You, O Supreme Goddess! O Rādhā, dearer than the life of Kṛṣṇa! O Form of supreme bliss!

I take shelter of Rādhikā, the beloved of the son of the King of Vraja. She has the luster of a spotless lotus, wears blue garments, has beautiful hair, a face like the moon, and eyes like a wagtail bird. She is charming, and shines with a pearl necklace on Her chest. She is an adolescent.

When will I be the sweeper of the courtyard of the play-grove of the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu, the ocean of rasa? The Supreme Person with a peacock-feather crown (Kṛṣṇa) always speaks many plaintive words to Her maidservants.

I remember with my mind the child Mukunda, who is placing His lotus foot with His lotus hand into His lotus mouth, sitting on the lap of the Queen of Vraja and smiling.

I praise Kṛṣṇa in the form of Gopāla, the son of Yaśodā, dark like a fresh raincloud, with eyes like blue lotuses.

I worship the child dark like a tamāla leaf, with restless eyes due to the blowing breath of the Queen of the Cowherds (Yaśodā), crawling on the earth with both knees, His lips like nectar with a slight fresh smile.

We meditate on the root of Brahman, the protector of the dynasty of the people of Vraja, the athlete Śrīla Gopāla. His very sweet pastimes, His habit of playing the flute, holding the hill in His palm; His dark limbs put to shame the blue raincloud.

Having abandoned sleep at dawn due to the sound of churning yogurt, entering the solitary room of the cowherd women, quickly extinguishing the lamps with the wind from His lotus mouth, having eaten the fresh butter—may the child Kṛṣṇa protect me.

Holding the string of small bells with His left hand to silence them, bending down, smiling gently, moving on tiptoes; forbidding the smiling girls in front of Him with a glance of His eyes, Hari stole the fresh butter from behind His mother.

Obeisances to the Lord of the brāhmaṇas, and to the benefactor of the cows and brāhmaṇas; to the benefactor of the world, to Kṛṣṇa, to Govinda, obeisances, obeisances.

To remove the fear of material existence, the author of the scriptures gathered the essence of knowledge and realization, the essence of the Vedas, like a bee; and made the servants drink the nectar from the ocean. I bow to the First Person, the Best, named Kṛṣṇa.

I worship Govinda, who is dedicated to playing the sweet flute, who is adorned with divine ornaments. His luster is like a blooming blue lotus, His face is like the moon, He is fond of the peacock feather ornament. He bears the Śrīvatsa mark, wears the large Kaustubha jewel, is dressed in yellow garments, and is beautiful. His body is worshiped by the lotus eyes of the gopīs, and He is surrounded by the herds of cows and cowherds.

Musk tilaka on the forehead, Kaustubha on the chest, a fine pearl on the tip of the nose, a flute in the palm of the hand, a bracelet on the wrist. Sandalwood paste on the whole body, a pearl necklace on the neck—surrounded by the cowherd women, the Crest-jewel of Cowherds is victorious.

Delightful with a peacock feather crown, with a musk tilaka, with cheeks touched by earrings. Lotus-eyed, conch-necked, with a face beautiful with a smile, with a flute placed on His lips. Dark, peaceful, three-fold bending, with garments like the sun's rays, adorned with a Vaijayantī garland. I bow to Him standing in Vṛndāvana, surrounded by hundreds of young women, in the dress of a brāhmaṇa-cowherd.

I praise the son of Nanda, the beloved of the gopīs, Śrī Govinda. Who is dark like a cloud, wearing supreme yellow garments.

Obeisances to Kṛṣṇa, to Kṛṣṇa⋅candra, sporting in Vṛndāvana. Obeisances to You, the Lord of the cowherd women; obeisances to the Husband of Rādhikā.

Obeisances to the son of the King of Cowherds, pleasing to millions of Cupids, with the luster of a blooming blue lotus, whose pastimes enchant the world.

I praise the son of Śrī Nanda, whose body is sweet rasa, who is full of the highest sweetness. Who has placed the flute on His moon-face, endowed with a smile, incomparable, wearing excellent yellow garments. Lotus-eyed, expert in everything, like a fresh cloud, with a peacock-feather crown, the Shelter. With a body curved in three places, accompanied by the young women of Vraja, the Hero who destroyed Keśī and others.

Obeisances to the lotus-eyed one, who delights in playing the flute. Who is accustomed to drinking the nectar of Rādhā's lips, who wears a forest garland.

Obeisances to Kṛṣṇa, Vāsudeva, Hari, the Supreme Soul. To the destroyer of the distress of the surrendered, to Govinda, obeisances, obeisances.

Meditate on Rādhikā and Kṛṣṇa⋅candra in the solitary grove of Cupid. Their bodies are like gold and a raincloud; Their eyes are like blue and red lotuses. Their foreheads are excellent with musk; They wear garlands of mālatī and kunda flowers. Their dress is trembling and youthful; They are the Lords of blue and yellow garments.

We meditate on Dāmodara, whose arm, bright with luster, is placed on Rādhā's shoulder. Who dims the blue lotus on all sides with the rays of the blackness of His body. Who defeats the luster of saffron with the beauty of His silk garment. Who resides in the forest of Vṛndā; who is fragrant with garlands shining on His chest.

All glories, all glories to this forest of Vṛndā of Murāri! Dearer than Vaikuṇṭha because of the presence of the very saintly ones. Where He always gives pleasure to the cows, protecting the gopīs; increasing love in the Rāsa dance with His sounding sweet flute.

I bow to that Vṛndāvana, the form of sweetness. Which is filled with the multitudes of bliss of Indirā (Rādhā). Where the son of Nanda is delighted by the multitudes of bliss. Which is delighted by the beautiful bride of Govinda.

Obeisances to You, O Holder of the Plow! Obeisances, O Wielder of the Club! Obeisances, O Husband of Revatī! Obeisances, O Affectionate to the Devotees!

Obeisances, O Best of the Strong! Obeisances, O Holder of the Earth! O Enemy of Pralamba, obeisances to You! Protect me, O Elder Brother of Kṛṣṇa!

Alas! This Hill, O friend, is the best of the servants of Hari! Because, being jubilant by the touch of the feet of Rāma and Kṛṣṇa, it offers respect to Them along with the cows and cowherd boys, with drinking water, good grass, caves, roots, and bulbs.

Victory to Govardhana, the King of the mountains! Who was declared by the gopīs to be the best servant of Hari. Who was worshipped by Kṛṣṇa to stop the sacrifice of Indra. Who resided on the palm of His lotus hand for seven days.

I bow my head to Govardhana Hill. Which was served by Hari with His own groups for seven days. Which became like a bee on the lotus hand of Acyuta; with fruits, roots, and caves.

We praise the divine lake (Rādhā-kuṇḍa) dear to the Mistress of That One (Kṛṣṇa) maddened in battle (of love). Which is always a mine of astonishment of great rasa by the sweet pastimes of the Endless Hari and Rādhikā. Which is greatly effulgent and most fragrant in Vṛndāvana.

O Śrī Sarovara (Lake)! My Mistress always plays here with Her Beloved with amorous sports. If You are thus dearer than the dear to Them, alas! show me Her, my life, today by mercy.

Just as Rādhā is dear to Viṣṇu, so is Her Kuṇḍa dear. Among all the gopīs, She alone is the most beloved of Viṣṇu.

I worship forever that dear lake (Śyāma-kuṇḍa). Which appeared from the lotus foot of Kṛṣṇa Himself at the killing of the wicked Ariṣṭa. Which is expanded like the spreading of honey; which is desired (iṣṭa) and named Ariṣṭa. Which is surrounded by steps made by Śrī Rādhā with affection. Which embraces the Lake of the Beloved (Rādhā-kuṇḍa) with love.

May the daughter of the Sun (Yamunā) purify us. She is the vessel of supreme love for the son of Sadānanda (Nanda), the sun of consciousness and bliss. Her body is liquid Brahman. She is the destroyer of sins, the giver of welfare to the world.

I bow to the Yamunā, delighted by Rādhā and Mukunda in the ocean of happiness. Whose lotus feet are served by the Ganges and other holy places. Who is great with the glory of the flood of the rasa of happiness of Goloka. Who has flooded everything with sweet water like nectar.

The Gaṅgā destroys sins immediately; She is always to be worshiped, the destroyer of misery. Giver of happiness, giver of liberation, the Gaṅgā is indeed the supreme destination.
